title: How to Create AI Content in GoHighLevel — Better Posts Faster
description: Learn how to create AI content in GoHighLevel using guided content questions. Generate platform-specific posts faster while maintaining brand voice.
html_content:
Creating content at scale is one of the biggest challenges agencies and businesses face. You need to post consistently across multiple platforms, maintain brand voice, and keep engagement high—but your team only has so many hours in the day.

That's where GoHighLevel's Content AI comes in. Built directly into the Social Planner, it uses guided content questions to help you generate platform-specific posts in minutes, not hours. No more staring at a blank screen. No more sacrificing quality for speed.

In this guide, I'll show you exactly how to use Content AI in GoHighLevel to create better posts faster while keeping your brand voice intact. And if you're ready to experience this yourself, start your free 30-day GoHighLevel trial here—that's double the standard trial.

📋 In This Guide

  • How Guided Content Questions Work in GoHighLevel
  • Step-by-Step: Creating Platform-Specific Posts with Content AI
  • Maintaining Brand Voice While Using AI
  • Advanced Tips for Higher-Quality Content
  • Why Content AI Saves Time Without Sacrificing Quality

How Guided Content Questions Work in GoHighLevel

GoHighLevel's Content AI isn't just a generic text generator. It uses guided content questions to understand what you're trying to say, who you're talking to, and where you're posting.

Here's what happens: When you open the Social Planner and select Content AI, the system walks you through a simple questionnaire. You answer things like:

  • What's the main message or topic?
  • Who is your target audience?
  • What action do you want readers to take?
  • Which platform are you posting to?
  • What's your brand tone (professional, casual, funny, etc.)?

Based on your answers, the AI generates content that's tailored to that specific platform's format and audience expectations. A LinkedIn post looks different from an Instagram post—and GoHighLevel's AI understands those nuances.

The beauty of this approach is that you're not just feeding the AI a generic prompt. You're giving it context. This means the output is more relevant, more on-brand, and more likely to drive engagement.

💡 Pro Tip

The more detailed your answers to the guided questions, the better your AI-generated content will be. Don't skip steps or give vague responses. Spend 2 minutes answering thoroughly, and you'll get a post worth publishing in 30 seconds.

Step-by-Step: Creating Platform-Specific Posts with Content AI

Step 1: Open the Social Planner

Log into your GoHighLevel account and navigate to the Social Planner from the main dashboard. This is where all your social management happens—scheduling, publishing, and analytics.

Step 2: Click "Create Post" and Select Content AI

Instead of starting from a blank post, select the "Content AI" option. This launches the guided workflow.

Step 3: Answer the Guided Content Questions

You'll see a series of questions appear. Here's what to include:

  • Topic: What's the post about? (e.g., "5 tips for improving email open rates")
  • Audience: Who should this reach? (e.g., "Marketing managers at SaaS companies")
  • Call-to-Action: What do you want people to do? (e.g., "Share in comments," "Click the link," "DM me")
  • Platform: Where are you posting? (LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, TikTok, etc.)
  • Brand Voice: How should it sound? Select from presets or describe your tone

Step 4: Generate and Review

Hit "Generate" and the AI creates a platform-optimized post. Review it for accuracy and brand alignment. You can regenerate if needed—get as many variations as you like.

Step 5: Edit and Schedule

Make any tweaks (add hashtags, adjust length, refine wording), then schedule for the optimal posting time. GoHighLevel's algorithm can recommend the best time to post based on your audience's activity.

Maintaining Brand Voice While Using AI

One common concern with AI-generated content is: "Will it sound like a robot? Will it lose my brand personality?"

The answer is no—if you use the tool correctly.

Here's how to keep your brand voice strong:

1. Define Your Brand Voice Upfront
Before you start generating content, take 10 minutes to describe your brand voice. Is it friendly and conversational? Professional and authoritative? Witty and irreverent? Write a few examples of how you typically communicate, and reference them as you work with Content AI.

2. Use the Brand Voice Setting in Content AI
GoHighLevel lets you set a default brand voice for your account. Do this once, and every piece of generated content will align with it. You can also override it on a per-post basis if needed.

3. Always Review and Edit Before Publishing
AI is a tool, not a replacement for human judgment. Spend 2-3 minutes reviewing generated content. Adjust phrasing, add personal touches, include specific examples or data from your experience. This is where your unique voice shines through.

4. Add Personal Touches and Context
AI creates a strong foundation, but you add the soul. Include a personal story, a specific example, or a unique insight that only you can provide. Readers connect with authenticity—and that authenticity is your responsibility to bring.

💡 Pro Tip

Create a "Brand Voice Guide" document within your GoHighLevel workspace. Include examples of your best-performing posts, your tone guidelines, and any phrases or messaging that are distinctly yours. Reference this when reviewing AI-generated content to ensure consistency.

Advanced Tips for Higher-Quality Content

Batch Create Content Weekly
Instead of creating posts one at a time, set aside 30 minutes weekly to batch-create 5-10 posts across all platforms. Use Content AI to generate variations, pick your favorites, edit them all at once, and schedule them out. This is exponentially more efficient than creating on-demand.

Use Content Themes and Pillars
Organize your content into 3-5 core themes (e.g., Education, Behind-the-Scenes, Customer Wins, Industry News). When answering Content AI's guided questions, reference these pillars. This ensures variety and prevents repetitive messaging.

Test and Optimize Based on Performance
GoHighLevel tracks engagement metrics on every post. Review which AI-generated posts perform best, and use those patterns to inform future prompts. What topics, formats, and calls-to-action drive the most clicks and comments? Lean into those.

Combine AI with User-Generated Content
AI excels at generating new ideas, but user-generated content builds community. Use Content AI to create posts asking followers to share their stories, then feature the best submissions. This blends automation with authentic engagement.

Why Content AI Saves Time Without Sacrificing Quality

Content creation is a time sink. Most businesses and agencies spend 5-10 hours per week just writing social posts. That's 250-500 hours per year.

With GoHighLevel's Content AI, you cut that to 2-3 hours per week. Here's why the quality actually improves:

1. Less Decision Fatigue
Writer's block kills productivity. By guiding you through a structured questionnaire, Content AI eliminates the blank-page problem. You're not staring at a cursor wondering what to say—you're answering specific questions, and the AI does the writing.

2. Consistent Posting Schedule
When content creation feels overwhelming, consistency suffers. You skip a day, then three days, then a week. With AI handling the heavy lifting, you can maintain a reliable posting schedule—which means better algorithm performance and more audience engagement.

3. Platform-Specific Optimization
Writing effective LinkedIn posts is different from writing TikTok captions. Content AI understands those differences and adapts automatically. You're not manually rewriting the same post for every platform; the AI does it for you.

4. More Bandwidth for Strategy
The time you save on writing goes toward strategy. You can spend more time on engagement (responding to comments, building relationships), analyzing performance, and refining your messaging approach.

The result: more posts, more engagement, better results—in less time.
